meta_title: Puppy Vaccinations Singapore: Exact Schedule | Curious Tails
meta_description: Singapore's puppy vaccination schedule, exactly: weeks 6, 8, 12 (optional 16), annual boosters, deworming at every visit, microchipping, and licensing requirements.
h1: Vaccination Schedule + Health Checks for Your New Puppy

entity_definitions.definition: Puppies in Singapore receive core vaccinations at weeks 6, 8, and 12, with an optional fourth at week 16 if the vet advises it, deworming at every vaccination visit, and an annual booster from year one onward. Puppies go home about a week after the second vaccination, around week 9, microchipped and ready for licensing.

top_competitor_weaknesses: schedules vary and confuse (6/8/12 vs other patterns)
nobody connects schedule → homecoming timing → licensing
factual_conflicts: various schedules online → resolution: use the canonical business-context block verbatim (6/8/12 +16 optional, annual booster)

all: Singapore Vaccination Guidelines for Dogs and Cats

faq.question: When do puppies get vaccinated in Singapore?
faq.answer_direction: Weeks 6, 8, and 12 for the core course, optional week 16 if the vet advises, annual booster from year one; deworming happens at every visit.

faq.question: Why can't a puppy come home before week 9?
faq.answer_direction: Homecoming is about a week after the second (week 8) vaccination so protection is building before the move's stress.

faq.question: Is microchipping compulsory?
faq.answer_direction: Yes, and it must match the licence; ours are microchipped before homecoming and licensing is completed at purchase.

faq.question: What should a vaccination record show?
faq.answer_direction: Dated entries for weeks 6 and 8 with vaccine names and the vet's stamp, plus the microchip number matching the puppy; missing pieces are a red flag.

faq.question: What does your pre-homecoming check cover?
faq.answer_direction: Vet check, vaccinations to schedule, deworming, and microchipping; records handed over at delivery. Said once, plainly.

faq.question: Who decides the optional week-16 shot?
faq.answer_direction: Your vet, based on the puppy and local risk; we flag the option, the vet makes the call.

remaining_risks: defer to vets on individual choices
